articles.title: VideoFlexTok: Flexible-Length Coarse-to-Fine Video Tokenization
articles.snippet: Visual tokenizers map high-dimensional raw pixels into a compressed representation for downstream modeling. articles.snippet: Maximum inner product search (MIPS) is a crucial subroutine in machine learning, requiring the identification of a vector taken within a database (the keys) that best aligns with a given query. articles.title: Residual Context Diffusion Language Models
articles.snippet: Diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs) have emerged as a promising alternative to purely autoregressive language models because they can decode multiple tokens in parallel. However, state-of-the-art block-wise dLLMs rely on a “remasking” mechanism that decodes only the most confident tokens and discards the rest, effectively wasting computation. articles.snippet: Reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) enable test-time scaling, with dataset-level accuracy improving as the token budget increases, motivating adaptive reasoning—spending tokens when they improve reliability and stopping early when additional computation is unlikely to help.
